shortDescription.text: Stored XSS via unsanitized memo content persisted in MongoDB
NoSQL injection via unvalidated memo parameter passed directly to MongoDB insert

message.text: The 'memo' parameter is inserted into MongoDB without any input sanitization or output encoding. Since memos are rendered as HTML via the marked library (as indicated by the surface description), an attacker can store arbitrary JavaScript in the memo field. When other users view the memo, the script executes in their browser context, enabling session hijacking, credential theft, or defacement.

The getAllMemos function retrieves all memos from MongoDB and returns them without any output encoding or sanitization. This is the read path that delivers stored XSS payloads to the rendering layer. Any malicious content previously stored via the insert function will be returned verbatim to the caller, which then renders it as HTML.

The 'memo' parameter is embedded directly into the MongoDB document without type checking or validation. If the caller passes a user-controlled object (e.g., from req.body) without ensuring it is a string, an attacker could inject MongoDB operators or manipulate the document structure. The lack of schema validation at the DAO layer means arbitrary fields or nested objects could be persisted.
